name: Patchak v. Zinke
question: Does a statute that directs the federal courts to “promptly dismiss” a pending lawsuit but that does not amend the underlying substantive or procedural laws violate the Constitution’s separation of powers principles?
description: A case in which the Court held that a statute that directs the federal courts to "promptly dismiss” certain lawsuits without affecting any underlying laws does not violate the Constitution’s separation of powers principles.
